stories.title: Trump leaves Beijing without clarity on Taiwan arms or Iran diplomacy
stories.summary: Donald Trump wrapped his China visit claiming progress with Xi Jinping, but the biggest strategic questions remain unresolved. Washington is still undecided on a major Taiwan arms package, while Beijing's value to any Iran endgame looks limited despite public talk of coordination.

links.headline: Trump weighs Taiwan arms package after summit aimed at steadying US-China ties
links.excerpt: AP focuses on Trump's post-summit hesitation over Taiwan weapons and the still-fragile relationship with Beijing.
links.imageAlt: Trump weighs Taiwan arms package after summit aimed at steadying US-China ties

links.headline: Trump leaves Beijing with few wins but warm words for Xi
links.excerpt: Reuters emphasizes the pageantry of the visit against the lack of concrete breakthroughs on trade, Iran and Taiwan.

links.headline: Trump departs China touting deals, but little clarity on Iran or Taiwan
links.excerpt: Al Jazeera pulls together the summit's unresolved geopolitical tensions and the limited payoff on the war-related agenda.
links.imageAlt: Trump departs China touting deals, but little clarity on Iran or Taiwan

stories.imageAlt: Trump weighs Taiwan arms package after summit aimed at steadying US-China ties

stories.title: Ukraine retaliates with one of its biggest drone strikes on Russia
stories.summary: Kyiv launched a massive drone barrage across Russian regions, including the Moscow area, after days of heavy Russian attacks on Ukraine. The exchange underlines how far both sides remain from any durable settlement despite repeated talk of ceasefires and diplomacy.

links.headline: Ukraine conducts large-scale drone strikes on Russia, killing 4 and wounding a dozen others
links.excerpt: AP tracks the casualties and the scale of the overnight strikes around Moscow and other regions.
links.imageAlt: Ukraine conducts large-scale drone strikes on Russia, killing 4 and wounding a dozen others

links.headline: Ukraine drones kill four in Russia, Moscow faces biggest attack in over a year
links.excerpt: Reuters highlights the retaliatory logic behind the attack after Russia's intense barrages on Kyiv.

links.headline: At least four people killed in Russia as Ukraine launches retaliatory strikes
links.excerpt: The Guardian adds detail on the size of the wave and the damage around Moscow's infrastructure.
links.imageAlt: At least four people killed in Russia as Ukraine launches retaliatory strikes

stories.imageAlt: Ukraine conducts large-scale drone strikes on Russia, killing 4 and wounding a dozen others

stories.title: Israeli attacks on Gaza continue after strike on Hamas military chief
stories.summary: Israel says it targeted the head of Hamas's military wing in Gaza, while fresh strikes continued elsewhere in the territory. The story matters both for any ceasefire track and for the worsening humanitarian picture, which remains contested across outlets and combatants.

links.headline: Gaza airstrike targeted Hamas military wing leader, Israel says
links.excerpt: AP leads with the Israeli claim that a top Hamas military figure was the intended target.
links.imageAlt: Gaza airstrike targeted Hamas military wing leader, Israel says

links.headline: Israeli strikes kill four people in Gaza, medics say
links.excerpt: Reuters centers the latest casualty reporting and the broader return to regular strikes after the Iran war front cooled.

links.headline: Three community kitchen workers among five killed by Israel in Gaza
links.excerpt: Al Jazeera focuses on the hit to community relief work and the Palestinian humanitarian framing.
links.imageAlt: Three community kitchen workers among five killed by Israel in Gaza

stories.imageAlt: Gaza airstrike targeted Hamas military wing leader, Israel says

stories.title: Lebanon truce is extended, but Israeli strikes continue almost immediately
stories.summary: The fragile Israel-Lebanon ceasefire has been extended, yet fighting and airstrikes have continued with little pause. That leaves diplomacy moving forward on paper while civilians absorb the reality that the truce still looks partial and brittle.

links.headline: New round of Lebanon-Israel talks kicks off as fighting between Israel and Hezbollah continues
links.excerpt: AP explains the Washington talks and the way daily fire has persisted despite the truce framework.
links.imageAlt: New round of Lebanon-Israel talks kicks off as fighting between Israel and Hezbollah continues

links.headline: Israeli strikes kill six in southern Lebanon hours after extension of ceasefire
links.excerpt: The Guardian captures how quickly violence resumed after the extension was announced.
links.imageAlt: Israeli strikes kill six in southern Lebanon hours after extension of ceasefire

links.headline: Israel kills five in Lebanon after 'ceasefire' with Hezbollah extended
links.excerpt: Al Jazeera provides local reporting context and the Lebanese casualty framing around the extension.
links.imageAlt: Israel kills five in Lebanon after 'ceasefire' with Hezbollah extended

stories.imageAlt: New round of Lebanon-Israel talks kicks off as fighting between Israel and Hezbollah continues

stories.title: WHO declares Congo-Uganda Ebola outbreak a global health emergency
stories.summary: The World Health Organization has designated the Ebola outbreak in the DRC and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern. The escalation reflects both the number of suspected cases and the worry that spread beyond the initial zones could outpace a difficult response.

links.headline: WHO declares global health emergency over Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda
links.excerpt: AP gives the latest case and death totals and explains why WHO stopped short of calling it a pandemic emergency.
links.imageAlt: WHO declares global health emergency over Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda

links.headline: Epidemic of Ebola disease in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda determined a public health emergency of international concern
links.excerpt: WHO's primary notice sets out the formal determination, transmission concerns and recommended response priorities.

links.headline: WHO says Ebola outbreak in DRC and Uganda is 'emergency' of international concern
links.excerpt: The Guardian adds broader public-health reaction and the concern about spread to major population centers.
links.imageAlt: WHO says Ebola outbreak in DRC and Uganda is 'emergency' of international concern

stories.imageAlt: WHO declares global health emergency over Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda

stories.title: Cruise-ship hantavirus cluster spills into wider public-health monitoring
stories.summary: The MV Hondius outbreak is still expanding into a cross-border health story as passengers are tested and quarantined after disembarkation. Public-health officials are balancing reassurance with a close look at transmission risks, especially because the outbreak involved a cruise environment and international repatriations.

links.headline: Canadian national health agency confirms 1 positive hantavirus test
links.excerpt: AP reports a newly confirmed case among returning cruise passengers and the wider monitoring effort.
links.imageAlt: Canadian national health agency confirms 1 positive hantavirus test

links.headline: Technical note for the disembarkation and onward management of passengers and crew in the context of an Andes virus-associated cluster on MV Hondius cruise ship
links.excerpt: WHO's technical note lays out the official handling guidance for passengers and crew from the affected ship.

stories.title: Karachi's extreme heat points to a harsher climate baseline in South Asia
stories.summary: Coverage from Pakistan and international outlets suggests extreme heat is becoming less exceptional and more structurally embedded in daily life. The concern is not just temperature records, but the interaction of heat with fragile utilities, water shortages and labor exposure.

links.headline: 'It's no longer exceptional': Karachi struggles under brutal new reality of extreme heat
links.excerpt: The Guardian frames Karachi's heat as a climate adaptation story rather than a one-off weather event.
links.imageAlt: 'It's no longer exceptional': Karachi struggles under brutal new reality of extreme heat

links.headline: 'A calamity': Why is a record heatwave sweeping South Asia?
links.excerpt: Al Jazeera zooms out to the regional scale and the role of climate change in amplifying the heatwave.
links.imageAlt: 'A calamity': Why is a record heatwave sweeping South Asia?

links.headline: Karachi to remain hot and humid but no chances of 'heatwave' for now
links.excerpt: Geo.tv adds local meteorological and utility context from inside Pakistan's largest city.
links.imageAlt: Karachi to remain hot and humid but no chances of 'heatwave' for now

stories.imageAlt: 'It's no longer exceptional': Karachi struggles under brutal new reality of extreme heat

stories.title: First auctions become an early field test of Labor's housing tax changes
stories.summary: The first Saturday auctions after the federal budget became a visible test of Labor's negative gearing and capital-gains changes. Supporters see a signal that the political system is finally tilting toward first-home buyers, while critics argue the supply side remains too constrained for a quick fix.

links.headline: First home buyers buoyed by negative gearing, capital gains changes at first Saturday auctions since budget
links.excerpt: ABC follows bidder behavior and sentiment at the first auction weekend after the budget shift.
links.imageAlt: First home buyers buoyed by negative gearing, capital gains changes at first Saturday auctions since budget

links.headline: Jim Chalmers' federal budget 2026: what we know so far about tax, housing, fuel and broken promises
links.excerpt: The Guardian provides broader context on the budget package and the political risk around the housing reform pitch.
links.imageAlt: Jim Chalmers' federal budget 2026: what we know so far about tax, housing, fuel and broken promises

links.headline: Budget 2026-27 overview
links.excerpt: The official budget document sets out the government's own framing for the housing and tax package.

stories.title: NDIS savings debate turns into a test of what states can rebuild
stories.summary: The budget's NDIS savings are shifting attention from headline fiscal numbers to the practical question of what state disability systems can absorb. The risk is that governments repeat older gaps in support if they cut federal growth faster than alternative services can be built.

links.headline: What do governments need to do this time to avoid pre-NDIS mistakes?
links.excerpt: ABC traces the pre-NDIS system and why advocates think the transition back to state-delivered supports is risky.
links.imageAlt: What do governments need to do this time to avoid pre-NDIS mistakes?

links.headline: Treasurer cites hard road of reform as he reveals 2026 federal budget
links.excerpt: SBS captures the government's argument that the savings are part of a broader reform agenda rather than simple retrenchment.
links.imageAlt: Treasurer cites hard road of reform as he reveals 2026 federal budget

links.headline: Budget 2026-27 overview
links.excerpt: The official budget overview is the primary source for the government's savings and reform language.

links.reliabilityBasis: source-prior=low; article-text: no reliability modifier cues

stories.title: Victoria's donation-law scramble exposes a pre-election integrity gap
stories.summary: Victoria is under pressure to rebuild its political donations regime after court setbacks weakened the existing framework. With an election approaching, the issue has shifted from legal technicality to whether the state can close obvious integrity vulnerabilities in time.

links.headline: Victoria's political donation system remains a free-for-all ahead of election
links.excerpt: ABC reports on the legislative gap and the political pressure to replace invalidated rules.
links.imageAlt: Victoria's political donation system remains a free-for-all ahead of election

links.headline: Judgment summaries and recent cases
links.excerpt: The court's recent-cases page is the primary reference point for the legal backdrop to Victoria's donation-law rethink.

stories.title: Senate chaos and a Marcos warning deepen the Philippines ICC crisis
stories.summary: The ICC case against Senator Ronald dela Rosa has become a wider institutional stress test for the Philippines, stretching from the Senate to the military sphere. President Ferdinand Marcos Jr's reminder that military loyalty belongs to the Constitution, not personalities, landed directly in that tense political atmosphere.

links.headline: Philippine senator wanted by the International Criminal Court flees from Senate
links.excerpt: AP details the escape from Senate protection and the way the ICC warrant has destabilized Manila politics.
links.imageAlt: Philippine senator wanted by the International Criminal Court flees from Senate

links.headline: Marcos to PMA grads: Loyalty to Constitution, not politics
links.excerpt: Philstar centers Marcos's speech to military graduates as the Dela Rosa crisis reverberates through the security establishment.
links.imageAlt: Marcos to PMA grads: Loyalty to Constitution, not politics

links.headline: Bato Dela Rosa listed as 'defendant' by ICC for crimes against humanity
links.excerpt: GMA adds the local legal framing and the domestic response around the unsealed ICC action.

stories.title: Chinese undersea device near Bali sharpens focus on maritime surveillance
stories.summary: Analysis of the Chinese-made device found in the Lombok Strait points to a broader contest over undersea awareness, submarines and strategic sea lanes. For Australia and regional partners, the story is less about one drifting object than about how far China's surveillance architecture may already extend.

links.headline: What the undersea monitoring device found in Indonesia tells us about China's maritime strategy
links.excerpt: ABC's analysis connects the device to China's wider effort to map and monitor strategic underwater routes.
links.imageAlt: What the undersea monitoring device found in Indonesia tells us about China's maritime strategy

links.headline: Indonesian fisherman nets surprise catch - a Chinese underwater drone
links.excerpt: SCMP provides additional reporting on the object itself and the strategic significance of the Bali-Lombok waterway.
links.imageAlt: Indonesian fisherman nets surprise catch - a Chinese underwater drone

links.headline: Chinese drone exposes Indonesia's 'insufficient' undersea capabilities
links.excerpt: A second SCMP report adds more on how Indonesian and regional analysts are reading the find as a capability gap story.
links.imageAlt: Chinese drone exposes Indonesia's 'insufficient' undersea capabilities

stories.imageAlt: What the undersea monitoring device found in Indonesia tells us about China's maritime strategy

stories.title: Canberra's AI approvals push is colliding with trust and oversight concerns
stories.summary: The federal government wants AI to help speed drug and housing approvals, but that promise immediately raises questions about transparency, accountability and error handling. The real test is whether productivity gains can be delivered without turning politically sensitive decisions into opaque automated workflows.

links.headline: The federal government wants to use AI to speed up drug and housing approvals. Can we trust it?
links.excerpt: ABC examines the accountability and governance questions around the budget's AI accelerator.
links.imageAlt: The federal government wants to use AI to speed up drug and housing approvals. Can we trust it?

links.excerpt: This earlier ABC report covers the budget mechanics behind the approvals push and its role in the housing agenda.
links.imageAlt: AI recruited to speed up home approvals, in budget set to reshape housing market

links.excerpt: The budget papers are the primary source for the government's AI productivity and approvals pitch.

stories.title: Chinese export curbs are exposing chemical and fertiliser weak points
stories.summary: China's sulphuric acid export restrictions are drawing attention to how vulnerable industrial and agricultural supply chains can become when one upstream input tightens. The issue is niche on the surface but important because it cuts across water treatment, mining, batteries, textiles and fertiliser.

links.headline: Amid disruption from Iran war, China quietly turns off sulphuric acid supply
links.excerpt: ABC Rural reports on the direct supply shock hitting Australian manufacturers and fertiliser users.
links.imageAlt: Amid disruption from Iran war, China quietly turns off sulphuric acid supply

links.headline: How gas shortages are hitting hard in Asia
links.excerpt: This background piece helps explain the broader energy and feedstock disruptions feeding into chemical-market stress.
links.imageAlt: How gas shortages are hitting hard in Asia

links.headline: Exposure of Australia's fertiliser deficit could inspire manufacturing comeback
links.excerpt: ABC adds the local strategic-manufacturing angle, especially around fertiliser dependence and resilience.
links.imageAlt: Exposure of Australia's fertiliser deficit could inspire manufacturing comeback

stories.title: Researchers and regulators are still learning from the cruise-ship outbreak
stories.summary: The hantavirus cluster is also a science and operations story about transmission uncertainty, outbreak management and how officials communicate risk under incomplete evidence. The ship case is becoming a live test of how global health systems handle a rare but high-attention pathogen.

links.headline: France says cruise ship Andes virus matches known South American viruses
links.excerpt: AP adds sequencing detail and the early indication that the detected virus does not appear unusually altered.
links.imageAlt: France says cruise ship Andes virus matches known South American viruses

links.excerpt: ABC shows how the outbreak is being handled operationally on the Australian side after repatriation.
